formal_strategy_baseline: ONE_SHOT_DONCHIAN_ATR_BREAKOUT 1.0.0 on BTCUSDT-PERP; related trend background, not a comparable replay
question: Can a fixed BTC/ETH/BNB monthly 180-day time-series momentum portfolio with 60-day volatility scaling remain profitable after actual funding and costs across sequential bull/bear periods while keeping risk suitable for a personal account?
rebalance: first UTC daily open of each month using prior completed daily close history
funding_rule: sum actual events in (daily open, next daily open]; positive rate debits longs and credits shorts
development_gate: data PASS; 180d base/stress >0; 2021/2022 each >0; max drawdown >-20% and no more than 2pp deeper than continuous 0.5x long; worst daily adverse >-15%; at least 2/3 lookbacks >0; turnover <=30
evaluation_gate: same with 2023/2024 each >0 and max drawdown >-18%
confirmation_gate: data PASS; base/stress >0; 2025/2026-to-Jun each >0; max drawdown >-18% and benchmark-relative limit; adverse >-15%; at least 2/3 lookbacks >=0; evaluation+confirmation compounded >0
conclusion_rule: SUPPORTS_WITHIN_SCOPE only after all sequential gates; negative confirmation or combined is DOES_NOT_SUPPORT; other failure is INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE.
data_release: fetch 2020-03..2023-01 development first; fetch 2022-03..2025-01 only after development; the extra ending month supplies the pre-registered inclusive funding event at each phase exit without extending analysis; fetch 2024-03..2026-06 only after evaluation
source: Binance USD-M official daily archives/checksums, public Kline gap fill, public funding history
known_exposure: BTC/BNB and other trend data were exposed in prior different rules; this exact three-core volatility-scaled portfolio and its freshly fetched phase manifests were not run before checkpoint. Holdouts are rule-level, while confirmation also extends to new 2026 data.
